model_output_raw_text: {"declared_mode":"indexing","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"OPTIMIZER = \"adamw\"","old":"OPTIMIZER = \"adam\"","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 1e-3","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 5e-4","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 5e-5","old":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 1e-4","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"indexing","rationale":"In a short 256-step regime, AdamW with a slightly larger learning rate should improve early optimization while keeping the edit simple and low risk. Reducing weight decay modestly avoids over-regularizing the small CNN before it has converged.","secondary_modes":["topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"DEPTH = 2","old":"DEPTH = 3","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 128","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 64","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"The depth increase did not beat the best result, so the next efficient test is to return to the stronger shallower model and use a larger batch. Under a fixed 256-step budget, a bigger batch exposes the optimizer to substantially more training examples and often improves short-horizon validation loss without adding code complexity.","secondary_modes":["layout"]}

{"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 256","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 128","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"The last improvement came from processing more examples within the fixed 256-step budget. Pushing batch size further is the simplest next extension of that successful trend and could move the run closer to full-dataset exposure within the verifier horizon.","secondary_modes":["topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 512","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 256","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"Increasing batch size again is the most direct extension of the strongest empirical trend so far. With a fixed 256-step budget, moving to 512 should let the model cover the training set multiple times and may further reduce validation loss without changing the optimizer or architecture.","secondary_modes":["topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 2e-3","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 1e-3","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"The optimizer is now training with a much larger batch than before, so the learning rate is likely the next bottleneck. A moderate LR increase is a simple, low-overhead follow-up that matches the successful large-batch regime without adding complexity or more wall time.","secondary_modes":["micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BASE_CHANNELS = 16","old":"BASE_CHANNELS = 12","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"After improving short-budget optimization with very large batches and a higher learning rate, the next plausible bottleneck is model capacity. A modest width increase is a simple architectural change that can improve representation quality without altering the training loop or introducing much extra complexity.","secondary_modes":["micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BASE_CHANNELS = 20","old":"BASE_CHANNELS = 16","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"The strongest gain so far came from increasing convolutional width, which suggests the model is still capacity-limited under the current optimization regime. A modest additional width increase is the simplest next test and keeps the successful training setup intact.","secondary_modes":["micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BASE_CHANNELS = 16","old":"BASE_CHANNELS = 20","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"FC_HIDDEN = 64","old":"FC_HIDDEN = 48","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"The extra convolutional width overshot, so the next sensible architecture test is to return to the stronger 16-channel trunk and increase only the classifier hidden size. That preserves the best-performing feature extractor while giving the larger pooled representation a bit more head capacity at low complexity.","secondary_modes":["micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"caching","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"FC_HIDDEN = 48","old":"FC_HIDDEN = 64","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 1e-5","old":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 5e-5","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"caching","rationale":"The larger classifier head did not help, so the next low-complexity test is to return to the stronger head size and relax weight decay. In the current high-batch, short-horizon regime, the model may still be underfitting rather than overfitting, making slightly weaker regularization a plausible improvement.","secondary_modes":["layout"]}

{"declared_mode":"indexing","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"ADAM_BETAS = (0.9, 0.99)","old":"ADAM_BETAS = (0.9, 0.999)","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"indexing","rationale":"The current setup is already strong, so the next low-cost optimization test is to make AdamW a bit more responsive in this large-batch regime by reducing beta2. A smaller second-moment timescale can help adaptation over the short verifier horizon without changing model size or runtime materially.","secondary_modes":["micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"ADAM_BETAS = (0.9, 0.999)","old":"ADAM_BETAS = (0.9, 0.99)","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 768","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 512","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"The beta change moved in the wrong direction, so the next strong hypothesis is to restore the more stable AdamW setting and continue the most successful trend: processing more examples within the fixed 256-step budget. A larger batch could further improve short-horizon fit by giving the model nearly four effective passes over the training set.","secondary_modes":["indexing"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 5e-5","old":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 1e-5","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 2.5e-3","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 2e-3","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 512","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 768","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"The larger batch overshot, so the next targeted test is to return to the strongest batch scale and regularization setting while nudging the learning rate upward. A small LR increase is a natural follow-up around the current best configuration and may extract a bit more progress from the widened model without extra architectural complexity.","secondary_modes":["micro","caching"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 3e-3","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 2.5e-3","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"The last gain came from a small upward learning-rate move while keeping the rest of the best configuration fixed. A further modest increase is the most direct follow-up and tests whether the current large-batch regime still benefits from more aggressive early optimization within the fixed 256-step horizon.","secondary_modes":["micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 2.5e-3","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 3e-3","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"CHANNEL_MULT = 3","old":"CHANNEL_MULT = 2","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"The higher learning rate overshot, so the next architecture test is to restore the better LR and shift capacity toward the later convolutional stage with a larger channel multiplier. That keeps the successful overall recipe intact while testing whether a stronger second block helps more than uniformly widening the whole network.","secondary_modes":["topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"CHANNEL_MULT = 2","old":"CHANNEL_MULT = 3","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BASE_CHANNELS = 18","old":"BASE_CHANNELS = 16","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"The larger second-stage expansion hurt, but the earlier width increase from 12 to 16 was the strongest architecture gain overall. The next sensible test is a milder capacity increase around the current best recipe: restore the better stage ratio and try a small width bump to 18 instead of the more aggressive settings that already failed.","secondary_modes":["micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BASE_CHANNELS = 16","old":"BASE_CHANNELS = 18","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"        self.pool = nn.AdaptiveAvgPool2d(1)
        self.classifier = nn.Sequential(
            nn.Flatten(),
            nn.Linear(in_ch, FC_HIDDEN),
            nn.ReLU(inplace=True),
            nn.Dropout(DROPOUT_RATE),
            nn.Linear(FC_HIDDEN, NUM_CLASSES),
        )","old":"        feat_size = IMAGE_SIZE // (2**DEPTH)
        feat_dim = in_ch * feat_size * feat_size
        self.classifier = nn.Sequential(
            nn.Flatten(),
            nn.Linear(feat_dim, FC_HIDDEN),
            nn.ReLU(inplace=True),
            nn.Dropout(DROPOUT_RATE),
            nn.Linear(FC_HIDDEN, NUM_CLASSES),
        )","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"        return self.classifier(self.pool(self.features(x)))","old":"        return self.classifier(self.features(x))","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"Incremental width tweaks around the current best have not helped, so the next worthwhile architecture test is to keep the stronger 16-channel trunk and simplify the head with global average pooling. That concentrates capacity in the convolutional features, sharply reduces the fully connected parameter burden, and can improve generalization in CIFAR-style CNNs without changing the overall training recipe.","secondary_modes":["caching"]}

{"declared_mode":"caching","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"        feat_size = IMAGE_SIZE // (2**DEPTH)
        feat_dim = in_ch * feat_size * feat_size
        self.classifier = nn.Sequential(
            nn.Flatten(),
            nn.Linear(feat_dim, FC_HIDDEN),
            nn.ReLU(inplace=True),
            nn.Dropout(DROPOUT_RATE),
            nn.Linear(FC_HIDDEN, NUM_CLASSES),
        )","old":"        self.pool = nn.AdaptiveAvgPool2d(1)
        self.classifier = nn.Sequential(
            nn.Flatten(),
            nn.Linear(in_ch, FC_HIDDEN),
            nn.ReLU(inplace=True),
            nn.Dropout(DROPOUT_RATE),
            nn.Linear(FC_HIDDEN, NUM_CLASSES),
        )","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"        return self.classifier(self.features(x))","old":"        return self.classifier(self.pool(self.features(x)))","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"    criterion = nn.CrossEntropyLoss(label_smoothing=0.05)","old":"    criterion = nn.CrossEntropyLoss()","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"caching","rationale":"The global-average-pooling head was clearly harmful, so the immediate priority is to restore the stronger flattened classifier. With that reversal in place, a small amount of label smoothing is a plausible low-complexity regularization tweak on top of the current best large-batch setup and could improve validation loss without changing runtime materially.","secondary_modes":["layout"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"    criterion = nn.CrossEntropyLoss()","old":"    criterion = nn.CrossEntropyLoss(label_smoothing=0.05)","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 2.4e-3","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 2.5e-3","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"Label smoothing was clearly harmful, so the final step should restore the stronger plain cross-entropy objective. With that reversal, a small learning-rate refinement around the current best setting is the highest-signal remaining test, since 2.5e-3 improved over 2e-3 while 3e-3 overshot.","secondary_modes":["caching"]}
